rationale: The citing paper explicitly discusses Ding (2007) in its methodological framing of conjoint bias and incentive compatibility. It uses Ding (2007) to explain that incentive-aligned conjoint is a recommended solution, but argues those practices were not practical in this launch context (new-to-market, nonstorable product), motivating its own hybrid RP-SP fusion approach. Although it does not reuse Ding's mechanism directly, the citation plays a meaningful role in positioning the paper's contribution as a complementary alternative when incentive alignment is infeasible.
relation_description: The citing paper draws on Ding (2007) as prior incentive-compatible conjoint work and then positions its own data-fusion method as a practical complement/alternative for settings where incentive-aligned conjoint cannot be implemented.
